utility_details.judge_error: In the wi_skills run, the agent invoked the /draft-outreach skill via a subagent task, which performed web research (visiting huggingface.co/osanseviero, searching HF blog posts) and identified Omar Sanseviero. However, despite this research, the final output document failed to commit to this identified name, instead using placeholders. The enrichment connector path was not successfully completed to retrieve a verified email. The verifier test_named_individual_present failed for both runs.
utility_details.utility_summary: wi_skills is moderately more effective than wo_skills. The wi_skills agent proactively researched the prospect (visited Omar Sanseviero's HF profile, searched blog posts, made multiple web requests) and produced a more structured outreach package with a value-first CTA (asking permission to send a 1-pager rather than directly requesting a call), a rationale section, and a cleaner LinkedIn message under character limits. The wo_skills agent produced a serviceable draft with good conversation-specific references and partnership framing but defaulted to a direct 20-minute call CTA without value-first framing, and performed no research at all. Both failed on prospect identity resolution (neither committed a specific name in the final output), enrichment data integration (no verified email), and published-content personalization. The wi_skills run scores 4/7 vs wo_skills 3/7, showing a meaningful but incomplete advantage from the skill.
